data.title: "Quantum-Resilient Security": Penny Stock SEALSQ (LAES) Jumps on New Protection Stack Launch
data.description:
Penny stock SEALSQ (LAES) surged over 7% on Wednesday after the post-quantum computing solutions provider said it is ready to offer its Quantum‑Resilient Vertical Security Stack to protect the next generation of quantum computers and the infrastructure that supports them.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

SEALSQ Reveals a Silicon-Level Security Stack

SEALSQ is rolling out a full security architecture built for quantum computing environments, combining four layers of protection into one system.

The stack includes hardware‑based Root of Trust, post‑quantum cryptography, secure semiconductor designs, and secure access tools for cloud‑based quantum computing. Together, these components protect devices, communications, chip-level electronics, and cloud services across the entire quantum ecosystem.

Also, these modules work together to form a quantum‑resilient security stack that protects everything from the silicon hardware to the cloud. Embedding security at the silicon level will allow developers to build systems that are secure from the start, avoiding the inefficiencies of adding security later.

What's Ahead for SEALSQ?

SEALSQ has been actively investing across the quantum technology landscape to strengthen its "Quantum Made in USA" strategy and expand its global footprint. Last month, the company invested in EeroQ. It is worth noting that EeroQ is developing a quantum‑chip architecture based on electrons on helium, and SEALSQ views it as a key partner in building secure quantum hardware.

The company's preliminary 2025 results show strong top‑line momentum but continued investment-driven losses. LAES reported $18 million in revenue, up 66% year over year, with $8 million generated in Q4 alone.

Looking ahead, the company projects 50%–100% year‑over‑year revenue growth for Q1 2026, supported by rising demand for quantum‑resilient security and the scaling of its post‑quantum product lineup.

Is LAES a Strong Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, LAES stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on two Buy ratings assigned in the past three months. The average SEALSQ stock price target of $7.50 implies 79% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: AVGO Earnings: Broadcom Stock Rises as Financial Results Top Wall Street Estimates
data.description:
Broadcom's (AVGO) stock is up after the chipmaker reported quarterly financial results that beat Wall Street estimates across the board.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Silicon Valley-based semiconductor maker announced earnings per share (EPS) of $2.05, which topped the $2.03 consensus forecast of analysts. Revenue of $19.31 billion beat the $19.18 billion that had been forecast on Wall Street. The company's sales were up 29% from a year earlier.

Looking ahead, Broadcom said that it expects a 68% margin for its earnings in the current quarter. Management also said they anticipate $22 billion in revenue this quarter. Analysts had sales of $20.56 billion penciled in for the company.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: RGTI Earnings: Rigetti Computing Stock Drops after Revenue Miss
data.description:

data.title: Stock Market Today Review: SPY, QQQ Recover Iran War Losses as Solid Economic Data Boosts Sentiment
data.description:
The S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed with solid gains on Wednesday. In addition, both ETFs have clawed back their losses from earlier this week as investors digested positive labor market and services data.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • ADP announced that private employers added 63,000 jobs in February, the most since July 2025 and topping the estimate of 50,000. 
  • The ISM Services Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) jumped to 56.1 in February, marking the highest level since July 2022 and above the estimate of 53.8.
  • President Trump officially nominated Kevin Warsh to replace Fed Chair Jerome Powell.
  • Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said that Trump will raise the global tariff rate to 15% from 10% "likely sometime this week."
  • Apple (AAPL) introduced the MacBook Neo, its most affordable laptop yet with a starting price tag of $599. On Tuesday, the tech giant announced new MacBook Pro and Air models equipped with M5 chips.
  • At the Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference, Nvidia (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang said that the company's $30 billion investment in OpenAI will likely be its last.
  • Wells Fargo analyst Aaron Rakers said the firm is a buyer of NVDA ahead of its GTC 2026 event from March 16‑19.
  • Tesla (TSLA) closed higher after Bank of America upgraded TSLA stock to "buy" from "hold" with a price target of $460.

Today's Best-Performing Sector 

Consumer discretionary was the best-performing sector on Wednesday, driven by easing inflation concerns stemming from falling energy prices. In addition, the sector tends to benefit when the stock market rises, which creates wealth for consumers and encourages spending. Upbeat private employment and services data also contributed to the rise.

Several consumer discretionary stocks led the sector's gains, including:

  • Tesla (TSLA)
  • Amazon (AMZN)
  • Expedia (EXPE)
  • Booking Holdings (BKNG)
  • Airbnb (ABNB)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, energy was the worst-performing sector as oil prices stabilized for the first time this week. At the same time, it's still the best performing sector year-to-date with a return of 25%.

On Tuesday, Trump directed the Development Finance Corporation to provide political risk insurance and financial security guarantees for ships crossing through the Persian Gulf, easing fears of oil supply disruption in the Strait of Hormuz. He also weighed sending U.S. naval escorts for oil and gas tankers in the passage.

Notable energy stocks trading lower include:

  • ConocoPhillips (COP)
  • Halliburton (HAL)
  • Chevron (CVX)
  • Exxon Mobil (XOM)
  • Occidental Petroleum (OXY)
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: RBC Capital Lowers Oracle (ORCL) Stock Price Target Ahead of Q3 Earnings. Here's Why
data.description:
Enterprise software and cloud computing company Oracle (ORCL) is scheduled to announce its results for the third quarter of Fiscal 2026 on March 10. ORCL stock has declined 22% so far in 2026 amid concerns about AI disruption, the company's high debt, and exposure to OpenAI. Heading into Q3 FY26 earnings, RBC Capital analyst Rishi Jaluria reiterated a Hold rating on Oracle and lowered his stock price target to $160 from $195, citing some concerns, including the company's ability to fund its Stargate commitments.

Meanwhile, Wall Street expects Oracle to report earnings per share (EPS) of $1.71, reflecting a 16.3% year-over-year growth. Meanwhile, revenue is projected to rise about 20% to $16.9 billion.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Ford February Sales Emerge, Ford Stock (NYSE:F) Notches Up
data.description:
Legacy automaker Ford (F) recently released its February sales figures, and the news was not as good as it might have been. Objectively, Ford sold plenty of vehicles, but it did not sell as many as it did this time last year. Investors took their wins where they could get them, and sent Ford shares up over 1.5% in Wednesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ford sold 149,962 vehicles in February. That is an impressive amount of sales, especially considering the average cost of one of these vehicles. Gas-powered cars did the best, with 55% more sales than the same time in 2025.

However, nearly every other class of vehicle was in decline, individually and corporately. Electric vehicle sales were down a disastrous 71%, with only 2,122 sold. Hybrid vehicle sales dropped 22%, hitting 12,010. Sport utility vehicle sales dropped 2.4%, truck sales dropped 9.4%, and all internal combustion vehicles lost 0.1% total.

A Call for Unity

Ford is the most "quintessentially American brand" in the country, which is something of a marvel when you consider all the other fundamentally American brands there are out there. But this position led Ford executive chair Bill Ford to use his position to call for Americans to unite and work together to build a stronger future.

It turns out that Ford is the most recognized brand between both Republicans and Democrats alike. It is also most recognized across all income levels. Ford himself noted, "As we look to the next 250 years, I believe the future is incredibly bright, but it hinges on us coming together as a nation. We must heal our divisions and keep pushing forward, just as we always have. Ford will be there, serving the nation and helping to build a stronger future."

Is Ford Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on F stock based on three Buys, 12 Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 31.61% rally in its share price over the past year, the average F price target of $14.02 per share implies 8.89% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: President Trump Discloses Netflix Bond Purchases During WBD Battle
data.description:
A new White House financial disclosure shows that President Donald Trump purchased between $600,000 and $1.25 million worth of Netflix (NFLX) debt in January. Notably, the filing indicates that these purchases came on top of another $500,000 to $1 million in Netflix bonds that he acquired in December. Although the investments were made earlier, the disclosure itself was signed by Trump on February 26, according to a report highlighted by The Hollywood Reporter.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Interestingly, a White House official explained that the investments are part of a portfolio strategy that's designed to follow established market indexes. As a result, the official emphasized that neither President Trump nor members of his family directly control how the portfolio is managed.

The Media Industry's Consolidation Battle

Separately, the media industry is dealing with a major consolidation battle. In particular, Paramount Skydance (PSKY) recently secured a deal to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) after a months-long bidding war that included Netflix as a competing bidder. Initially, Netflix reached a cash-and-stock agreement valued at about $27.75 per share for key Warner assets in early December.

However, Paramount later increased its hostile offer by raising the price and by adding protections, such as quarterly cash payments if the deal closes after 2026. It also agreed to cover Netflix's multibillion-dollar termination fee. Ultimately, the final agreement was reached at $31 per share, with the transaction expected to close in the third quarter of 2026.

Is Netflix a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on NFLX stock based on 30 Buys, nine Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average NFLX price target of $114.26 per share implies 15.8% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Oil Prices Jump after Iran Claims Control of Strait of Hormuz — 3 Top Energy Stocks to Watch Now
data.description:
Oil prices moved higher after Iran's Revolutionary Guard said it has taken control of the Strait of Hormuz and warned ships not to pass through the route. The development raised new concerns about global oil supply and increased fears of a wider conflict in the Middle East.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Following the development, Brent crude prices extended a four-day rally, rising 2.65% to $83.60 per barrel on Wednesday, hovering near the highest level since January 2025. U.S. West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude also climbed for a third straight session to $76.45. The rise in prices is putting major oil producers such as Devon Energy (DVN), SLB (SLB) and Diamondback Energy (FANG) in focus.

Why the Strait of Hormuz Matters

Iran lies next to the Strait of Hormuz, a narrow waterway that carries a large share of the world's oil shipments. The route handles a significant portion of global crude exports, making it one of the most important chokepoints in the energy market.

Because so much oil passes through this corridor, any threat to tanker traffic can quickly push prices higher. Analysts said that if tanker traffic remains limited, oil prices could stay elevated in the near term. As a result, we identified two "Strong Buy" energy companies using the TipRanks database that could benefit from the rise in oil prices.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: U.S.-Iran War Presents a 'Buying Opportunity,' Says Goldman Sachs
data.description:
The start of the U.S.-Iran war has reintroduced volatility into the stock market, although the underlying strength of the economy and healthy corporate earnings should reverse any short-term losses, according to strategists at Goldman Sachs.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"We see correction risks as high given current valuations, but expect this to present a buying opportunity with relatively low risk of a more protracted and deep bear market," said Goldman Chief Global Equity Strategist Peter Oppenheimer.

U.S.-Iran War Poses Energy, Inflation Risks

Oppenheimer noted that market risks will correlate with the length of the war, as a longer conflict could disrupt the supply of energy and boost inflation. Iran borders the Strait of Hormuz, which handles roughly 20% of global oil flows. Volume at the strait has plunged by 90% since the U.S. and Israel struck Iran over the weekend.

Furthermore, recent geopolitical shocks haven't resulted in a "long-lasting impact" on the market, said Oppenheimer. Separate data from Carson Investment Research shows that since World War II, the S&P 500 (SPX) has returned an average of 0.8% three months after a major geopolitical event and 3.0% six months after.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Having Fired 57,000 Humans, Amazon (AMZN) Now Sets Its Sights on the Company's Robots
data.description:
Amazon isn't just firing its human workers. The technology giant is also scaling back its robots.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Seattle-based e-commerce company has announced that it is eliminating positions in its robotics division as part of a cost-cutting effort and desire to scale back its reliance on robots in its warehouse and delivery units.

The latest cuts come after Amazon has gutted its global workforce by 57,000 roles since late 2022. Scott Dresser, vice president of Amazon Robotics, informed employees on March 4, reportedly saying that the cuts are difficult but necessary. He said the company's robots are being restructured amid cost pressures.

The job cuts come weeks after Amazon announced that it had suddenly stopped development of its highly anticipated "Blue Jay" robot project months after launching it. Project Blue Jay was a multi-armed robotic system that Amazon planned to develop and deploy at its same-day delivery warehouses.

Who Will Replace Who?

The exact number of employees impacted by the robotics division layoffs hasn't been disclosed. However, analysts have been quick to say that the cuts mark a dramatic reversal for Amazon, which has largely staked its future on using a growing army of robots to both fulfill e-commerce orders in its warehouses and deliver packages to homes and businesses.

Multiple media reports say that an Amazon spokesperson confirmed the company eliminated roles in its robotics unit this week. The latest cuts follow previous layoffs announced in October and January. CEO Andy Jassy has said several times that he is trying to reduce bureaucracy within Amazon.

Is AMZN Stock a Buy?

Amazon stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 44 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 41 Buy and three Hold recommendations assigned in the last three months. The average AMZN price target of $282.14 implies 40.73% upside from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on AMZN stock
data.author: Joel Baglole
